behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure occurs at the boundary between the module that fetches the changed-file list (from GitHub API) and the module that dispatches files to the linter. A unit test of pure filtering logic wouldn't catch bugs where the wrong list is passed across that boundary. REAL-INFRA: The GitHub API call can be satisfied by a mock HTTP client — an in-process fake reproduces the exact failure (wrong files analyzed) without real network. Not System. LLM-DEP: None. REJECTION-GATE for Unit: if the file-selection logic is exercised as a pure function in isolation, a bug in how the API response is wired into the analyzer loop would not be caught — that disqualifies Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the GitHub changed-files API client (mock HTTP returning a known list of 3 changed and 2 unchanged files) to the analysis dispatcher. Assert that the linter is invoked exactly for the 3 changed files and never for the 2 unchanged ones.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is entirely within the grouping/formatting algorithm — given a flat list of findings with severity tags, produce a grouped comment string. No module boundary is crossed; the bug would appear in a single function's output. REAL-INFRA: No OS, network, or subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing to mock — the function takes data in and produces text out. LLM-DEP: None. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: there is no handoff between modules whose interaction could cause this specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the comment-formatting function directly with a pre-built list of findings spanning all three severities. Assert the output string contains each severity section and places findings under the correct heading.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: This is a wall-clock performance constraint tied to real subprocess execution, real filesystem I/O across 200 files, and process-spawn overhead. An in-process fake subprocess would eliminate the very overhead that causes the timeout violation — mocks would always pass regardless of actual performance. The failure only manifests with real subprocesses and real files on a real OS. MOCK-HIDE: Any mocked linter call completes in microseconds and hides the real timing failure. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: an integration test with a stubbed subprocess cannot reproduce the I/O and process-spawn latency that drives the actual timeout risk.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp directory with 200 minimal source files (e.g., syntactically valid but varied). Invoke the full CI action script as a subprocess, measure elapsed time, and assert it completes in under 10 minutes. Run on a machine representative of GitHub Actions runners.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure occurs at the boundary between the subprocess invocation layer and the orchestration loop — specifically whether the loop catches the crash and continues. A unit test of the loop in isolation with a simple exception mock is plausible, but the bug is more likely in how the module wires subprocess error handling into the continuation logic. REAL-INFRA: The crash can be faithfully simulated by a stub subprocess that raises CalledProcessError or returns a non-zero exit code on a designated 'malformed' file — the in-process fake reproduces the exact failure mode (loop stops vs. continues). Real process signals or pipe-break nuances are not what this behavior is asserting. REJECTION-GATE for System: the behavior does not specify signal-level crashes, broken pipes, or platform-specific termination behavior that would require a real process; a stub crash reproduces the exact control-flow failure.
behaviors.test_description: Using a stub subprocess wrapper, configure the linter invocation to raise CalledProcessError for one specific 'malformed' input file and succeed for all others. Assert that analysis results are collected for every non-malformed file and that the final comment-posting call is still made.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is entirely within the deduplication algorithm — given a collection of findings, identify and remove entries that share the same (file, line, message/rule) key. No module boundary is involved; the bug lives inside a single function or data-structure operation. REAL-INFRA: No I/O required. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing to mock. LLM-DEP: None. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: there is no inter-module handoff whose interaction could cause this specific failure — the deduplication logic is self-contained.
behaviors.test_description: Call the deduplication function with a list containing intentional duplicates (same file, same line, same rule from two overlapping rules) plus unique findings. Assert the output contains exactly one entry per unique (file, line, rule) tuple and that no unique findings are dropped.
